creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
Re: <https://docs.freebsd.org/en/books/handbook/x11/#x-fonts>

Quoting Simon Adameit under <https://discord.com/channels/727023752348434432/760416197803245591/1057953404892217455>, with his permission.

> … based on what I've heard here, I'd suggest to:
> 
> - make the introduction to fonts less dependend on X: e.g. introduce 
>   fontconfig and communicate what the consumers of it are (e.g. gtk, qt) 
>   such that the docs can be useful even if the user does use wayland
> 
> - get rid of legacy suggestions to install e.g. urwfonts and maybe 
>   replace those with whatever the "modern" OpenType replacement of those is 
> 
> In general I prefer documentation that doesn't just give a recipe but also 
> helps understand the different components and concepts involved and how 
> they fit together such that I can gain more general knowledge that can be 
> adapted to my specific setup and goals.
